response_anchor: 0 = strongly disagree ... 4 = strongly agree (placeholder; final scoring TBD)
diff_from_v1: 5 items revised: GHI-01, GHI-03, GHI-10, GHI-15, GHI-18 — each now includes an explicit distress, impairment, escalation, or loss-of-control marker per the King et al. (2020) v1-audit feedback.

items.label: Consumable hoarding (distress at unused)
items.stem: I have finished a game with rare consumable items completely unused, and the realisation that I never deployed them caused me real disappointment or regret about how I played.
items.revised_from_v1: Added distress marker ('real disappointment or regret') and outcome marker ('completely unused' over the full game).
items.v1_stem: I save powerful consumable items in my game inventory for a moment of need that rarely comes.

items.label: Consumable hoarding (distress at use)
items.stem: Using a rare consumable item makes me feel that I have lost something valuable, even when the use was strategically sound.

items.label: Gacha pursuit (escalation + loss-of-control)
items.stem: I have spent more on gacha or loot-box pulls in pursuit of a complete set than I had originally intended in that session, and recognised at the time that I felt unable to stop until the set was complete.
items.revised_from_v1: Added escalation marker ('more than originally intended') and loss-of-control marker ('felt unable to stop').
items.v1_stem: I have spent money on randomised in-game purchases (loot boxes, gacha pulls) primarily to complete a set or roster rather than to obtain a single specific item.

items.label: Gacha pursuit (limited-time)
items.stem: Fear of missing a limited-time in-game offer has caused me to spend more money on a game than I planned.

items.label: Gacha pursuit (loss of control)
items.stem: After a disappointing gacha pull or loot-box result I have continued spending in the same session to recover from the loss.

items.label: Gacha pursuit (financial impairment)
items.stem: Spending on in-game purchases has, at least once, interfered with money I needed for rent, food, bills, or savings.

items.label: Inventory paralysis (discard difficulty)
items.stem: I find it difficult to delete, sell, or destroy in-game items even when my inventory is overfull and the items have no current use.

items.label: Inventory paralysis (storage workaround)
items.stem: I maintain alternate characters, paid storage, or external storage purely to avoid discarding items I might never use again.

items.label: Inventory paralysis (time impairment)
items.stem: I have spent more than an hour in a single session organising my game inventory rather than playing the game.

items.label: Crafted item attachment (escalation + impairment)
items.stem: I have continued to keep crafted or upgraded items I no longer use to the point where my inventory or paid storage was full, and chose to expand storage rather than discard the older crafted items.
items.revised_from_v1: Added escalation marker ('to the point where storage was full') and behavioural impairment marker ('chose to expand storage rather than discard').
items.v1_stem: I would refuse to discard a self-crafted or self-upgraded item even when a functionally superior replacement is available.

items.label: Completionism (resumed games)
items.stem: I have returned to a game I no longer enjoy purely to finish 100% completion, all achievements, or every collectible.

items.label: Completionism (distress at incompleteness)
items.stem: Leaving a game's achievement list or collection incomplete causes me persistent discomfort that interferes with my enjoyment of other games.

items.label: Completionism (compulsive resumption)
items.stem: Once I start a collection-based system in a game, I feel I cannot stop until it is finished, regardless of whether finishing is fun.

items.label: Completionism (achievement chasing)
items.stem: I have purchased a game I had no interest in playing primarily so that I could earn its achievements.

items.label: Backlog accumulation (escalation + distress)
items.stem: I have continued to buy heavily discounted games during sales even when my unplayed library already causes me ongoing guilt or anxiety, telling myself I might want them later.
items.revised_from_v1: Added existing distress marker ('ongoing guilt or anxiety') and escalation marker ('continued to buy ... even when'). The continued-acquisition-despite-distress framing is the load-bearing fix.
items.v1_stem: During digital-storefront sales I have bought games I knew I would probably not play, because I felt I might want them later.

items.label: Backlog accumulation (distress at growth)
items.stem: My unplayed-game library has grown to a size that causes me ongoing guilt or anxiety, yet I continue to add to it.

items.label: Loss aversion on virtual resources
items.stem: Spending an in-game resource (currency, charges, ammo) feels meaningfully painful even when the resource has zero real-world value and can be replenished.

items.label: Emotional attachment (impairment)
items.stem: The number of sentimentally-significant but functionally-useless items I keep in my game inventories causes me ongoing inventory management difficulty (paid storage expansions, overflow problems), yet I continue to retain them.
items.revised_from_v1: Added functional impairment marker ('ongoing inventory management difficulty', 'paid storage expansions, overflow problems') and continued-behaviour-despite-impairment marker ('yet I continue to retain them').
items.v1_stem: There are items in my game inventories that I would not trade away or destroy because of personal memories attached to them, even though they are functionally useless.
